rulings.Case Title: MELVIN MCMINN VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice-Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 27, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Brayton Purcell LLP's motion to be relieved as counsel for plaintiff Melvin McMinn is granted. Plaintiff received proper notice of this motion and no opposition was filed. Hearing required unless Brayton Purcell informs Mr. McMinn of this tentative ruling and he states that he does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Brayton Purcell must bring a proposed order to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ERNEST MOUTON (PROVIDE ACCESS) VS. GOODRICH CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel - Civil
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 27, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Brayton Purcell LLP's motion to be relieved as counsel is continued on the court's own motion to August 20, 2015 pursuant to Mr. Mouton's objection to the motion and request to be present at the hearing. A continuance is required so that preparations may be made for Mr. Mouton, who is in custody, to appear telephonically at the hearing. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY SUBLETT VS. KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 27, 2015 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Chicago Bridge and Iron Company's motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party's request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY SUBLETT VS. KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT By Defendant Cupertino Electric Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 27, 2015 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Cupertino Electric, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. However, even if it had sustained its burden, Robert Cantley's declaration would create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (fireproofing) attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY SUBLETT VS. KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Of Defendant Atlas Heating And Ventilating Company, Ltd.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 27, 2015 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Atlas Heating and Ventilating Company, Ltd.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. However, even if it had sustained its burden, Robert Cantley's declaration would create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (fireproofing) attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
